title: 帷幕
summary:
Hastings realizes that the shooting at Styles Manor follows a pattern involving a mysterious figure known as "X." He recalls previous cases where individuals, such as the tenant farmer Riggs or Maggie Richmond, believed they had committed murders that they actually had not. The next morning, Hastings shares this realization with Poirot, who confirms the connection but refuses to disclose specific details or motives to protect Hastings from the culprit. Poirot warns that the recent shooting was only avoided by luck and that a murderer is likely to strike again.

Hastings remains suspicious of the guests and attempts to test his theory regarding Captain Alton. He questions Judith about her whereabouts the previous night, specifically asking if Alton was with her. Judith becomes defensive and angry, claiming she went to town alone to buy stamps and that she and Alton only met briefly near the house before running into Hastings. Hastings observes the interaction between Judith and her employer, John Franklin, noting that Franklin treats her with an arrogant and superficial manner that contrasts with Alton's excessive attention.

Hastings fears that Alton may be "X," noting that Alton was not with Judith when the gun went off and observing that Alton is a rational man lacking moral boundaries. Boyd Caldwell provides further information, revealing that Alton is married to a devout Roman Catholic who refuses to divorce him, a fact Caldwell suggests makes Alton appear more reliable than he truly is. Meanwhile, at the hospital, Mrs. Franklin expresses resentment toward the attention being given to Lieutenant Lestrade's wife.
